seed.topic: Long-running AI agents for scientific computing
seed.goal: Study when long-horizon coding and compute agents help scientists, how errors accumulate over extended runs, and which oversight structures keep results trustworthy.
seed_questions: Which verification checkpoints most reduce silent error accumulation in long-running scientific-computing workflows?
Which scientific tasks benefit from long-running autonomy, and which still require tighter staged human review?

seed_definitions.name: long-running agent
seed_definitions.meaning: An AI system that plans, executes, and revises work over an extended time horizon rather than a single prompt-response turn.

seed_definitions.name: scientific computing workflow
seed_definitions.meaning: A research workflow involving code, data, simulation, numerical analysis, or experiment orchestration in service of scientific work.
